benchmark_details.name: VIDHALLUC
benchmark_details.overview: VIDHALLUC is the largest benchmark designed to examine hallucinations in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) for video understanding, assessing hallucinations across three dimensions: action, temporal sequence, and scene transition using 5,002 videos and 9,295 QA pairs.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To evaluate hallucinations in Multimodal Large Language Models during video understanding tasks.

data.source: Automated data collection pipeline utilizing existing video description datasets, including ActivityNet, YouCook2, and VALOR32K.
data.annotation: Automatically generated questions verified by human annotators.

methodology.calculation: Accuracy is calculated as the number of correctly answered questions divided by the total number of questions.
methodology.interpretation: Higher accuracy indicates better performance in identifying actions, sequences, and scene transitions.
methodology.validation: Extensive testing shows that most MLLMs are vulnerable to hallucinations across key dimensions.
